title: cerastium malyi subsp. malyi
intro: Cerastium malyi subsp. malyi, also known as the Tibetan chickweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium malyi subsp. malyi include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
